title: Estilo de vida
goals: Simple present, routines and activities, jobs, time.
Adverbios de frecuencia, have/has, la hora.

aspects.title: En la cafetería
aspects.summary: Pedir permiso, preguntar por el trabajo, los horarios y la rutina.

aspects.title: Presente simple: afirmativo
aspects.summary: Rutinas y hechos. Tercera persona con -s / -es.

aspects.title: Presente simple: preguntas
aspects.summary: Preguntar con do/does y con be, y respuestas cortas (Yes, I do / Yes, I am).

aspects.title: Adverbios de frecuencia
aspects.summary: always…never, y dónde se ubican en la oración.

aspects.title: La hora
aspects.summary: Preguntar y decir la hora con at.

aspects.title: have / has
aspects.summary: Posesión: have con I/you/we/they, has con he/she/it.

aspects.title: Rutinas y actividades
aspects.summary: Vocabulario del día a día: levantarse, desayunar, salir de casa…

aspects.title: Preguntas con Wh-
aspects.summary: What, when, who, where, what time + do/does.

atoms.text: Can I sit here?
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: kæn aɪ sˈɪt hˈɪɹ

atoms.text: Yes, please.
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
atoms.ipa: jˈɛs plˈiːz

atoms.text: Do you work here?
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
replies: Yes, I do. What about you?
atoms.ipa: dˈuː juː wˈɜːk hˈɪɹ

atoms.text: Yes, I do. What about you?
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
atoms.ipa: jˈɛs aɪ dˈuː wˌʌt ɐbˈaʊt juː

atoms.text: I work here too.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: aɪ wˈɜːk hˈɪɹ tˈuː

atoms.text: What do you do?
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
alternatives: What do you do for a living?
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt dˈuː juː dˈuː

atoms.text: I design programs.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: aɪ dɪzˈaɪn pɹˈoʊɡɹæmz

atoms.text: What time do you start working?
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
atoms.ipa: wˌʌt tˈaɪm dˈuː juː stˈɑːɹt wˈɜːkɪŋ

atoms.text: At eight o'clock.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: æɾ ˈeɪt əklˈɑːk

atoms.text: Do you always start at eight o'clock?
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
atoms.ipa: dˈuː juː ˈɔːlweɪz stˈɑːɹt æɾ ˈeɪt əklˈɑːk

atoms.text: No, I sometimes start at seven o'clock.
atoms.gloss: No, a veces empiezo a las siete.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: nˈoʊ aɪ sˈʌmtaɪmz stˈɑːɹt æt sˈɛvən əklˈɑːk

atoms.text: Does the cafeteria open at seven o'clock?
atoms.speaker: cafe-woman
replies: No, it doesn't. It opens at eight.
atoms.ipa: dˈʌz ðə kˌæfɪtˈɪɹiə ˈoʊpən æt sˈɛvən əklˈɑːk

atoms.text: No, it doesn't. It opens at eight o'clock.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: nˈoʊ ɪt dˈʌzənt ɪɾ ˈoʊpənz æɾ ˈeɪt əklˈɑːk

atoms.text: I usually wait one hour to have my coffee.
atoms.gloss: Normalmente espero una hora para tomar mi café.
atoms.speaker: cafe-man
atoms.ipa: aɪ jˈuːʒuːəli wˈeɪt wˈʌn ˈaʊɚ tə hæv maɪ kˈɔfi

atoms.title: En la cafetería
atoms.situation: Dos empleados coinciden en la cafetería de la empresa y hablan de su trabajo y sus horarios.

atoms.prompt: When do you go to discos?
atoms.speaker: student

replies: Yes, full time. I work part time.

replies: Five days a week, five hours a day.

replies: In Elmhurst. It's in New York.

atoms.text: Do you take credit cards?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: dˈuː juː tˈeɪk kɹˈɛdɪt kˈɑːɹdz

atoms.text: I have breakfast at seven.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ hæv bɹˈɛkfəst æt sˈɛvən

atoms.text: She goes running every morning.
atoms.gloss: Ella sale a correr todas las mañanas.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː ɡoʊz ɹˈʌnɪŋ ˈɛvɹi mˈɔːɹnɪŋ

atoms.text: They leave home at eight.
atoms.gloss: Salen de casa a las ocho.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðeɪ lˈiːv hˈoʊm æɾ ˈeɪt

source.note: Oración de contexto para "meet new people".

atoms.text: I meet new people at work.
atoms.gloss: Conozco gente nueva en el trabajo.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ mˈiːt nˈuː pˈiːpəl æt wˈɜːk

atoms.text: Venus wakes up at seven in the morning.
atoms.gloss: Venus se despierta a las siete de la mañana.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: vˈiːnəs wˈeɪks ˌʌp æt sˈɛvən ɪnðə mˈɔːɹnɪŋ

atoms.text: She has a shower and brushes her teeth.
atoms.gloss: Se ducha y se lava los dientes.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː hɐz ɐ ʃˈaʊɚ ænd bɹˈʌʃᵻz hɜː tˈiːθ

atoms.text: She always goes walking in the park.
atoms.gloss: Siempre sale a caminar por el parque.
atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.text: She usually eats fish for dinner.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː jˈuːʒuːəli ˈiːts fˈɪʃ fɔːɹ dˈɪnɚ

atoms.text: She never goes to bed after eleven.
atoms.gloss: Nunca se acuesta después de las once.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː nˈɛvɚ ɡoʊz tə bˈɛd ˈæftɚɹ ᵻlˈɛvən

source.note: Refuerza have/has (posesión), tema flaco en la U2.

atoms.text: She has two brothers.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ʃiː hɐz tˈuː bɹˈʌðɚz

atoms.text: I have a new computer.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ hæv ɐ nˈuː kəmpjˈuːɾɚ

atoms.text: We have fun at the party.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: wiː hæv fˈʌn æt ðə pˈɑːɹɾi

atoms.text: We are fit because we go running.
atoms.gloss: Estamos en forma porque salimos a correr.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: wiː ɑːɹ fˈɪt bɪkˈʌz wiː ɡˌoʊ ɹˈʌnɪŋ

atoms.text: I listen to music every day.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: aɪ lˈɪsən tə mjˈuːzɪk ˈɛvɹi dˈeɪ

atoms.text: Would you like the mountain tour?
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: wʊd juː lˈaɪk ðə mˈaʊntɪn tˈʊɹ

atoms.text: Bill studies mathematics all of the time. He always studies.
atoms.gloss: Bill estudia matemática todo el tiempo. Siempre estudia.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: bˈɪl stˈʌdiz mˌæθɪmˈæɾɪks ˈɔːl ʌvðə tˈaɪm hiː ˈɔːlweɪz stˈʌdiz

atoms.text: John almost never studies. He seldom studies.
atoms.gloss: John casi nunca estudia. Rara vez estudia.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: dʒˈɑːn ˈɔːlmoʊst nˈɛvɚ stˈʌdiz hiː sˈɛldəm stˈʌdiz

atoms.text: The students are always at the university.
atoms.gloss: Los estudiantes siempre están en la universidad.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə stˈuːdənts ɑːɹ ˈɔːlweɪz æt ðə jˌuːnɪvˈɜːsᵻɾi

atoms.text: The teacher is never in the cafeteria.
atoms.gloss: El profesor nunca está en la cafetería.
atoms.speaker: narrator
atoms.ipa: ðə tˈiːtʃɚɹ ɪz nˈɛvɚɹ ɪnðə kˌæfɪtˈɪɹiə

atoms.prompt: Describe someone's daily routine, like Venus's. Use the third person (-s) and frequency adverbs.
atoms.modelAnswer: Venus wakes up at seven in the morning. She gets up and has a shower. She brushes her teeth and has breakfast. She always goes walking in the park. At eleven, she plays tennis with her sister. She goes back home at one and has lunch. In the afternoon she plays tennis again. She has dinner with her family and she usually eats fish. She never goes to bed after eleven.
modelVariants: My father gets up at six. He has a coffee and reads the news. He goes to work by train and he always starts at eight. He has lunch in the canteen. He usually finishes at five. In the evening he watches TV and he sometimes goes running. He goes to bed at eleven.

My sister wakes up late. She never has breakfast at home. She studies medicine and she goes to the university every day. She often meets her friends in the afternoon. She likes music and she listens to music at night. She goes to bed after midnight.

rubric.text: Usa la tercera persona con -s (wakes, has, goes)
Usa adverbios de frecuencia (always, usually, never…)
Describe varias acciones de la rutina en orden

atoms.speaker: narrator

atoms.prompt: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb (smoke, meet, close, teach, go, like).

items.stem: She’s very clever. She speaks four languages.
Steve ___ twenty cigarettes a day.
The UAI university ___ at midnight.
Tina is a teacher. She ___ mathematics to young children.
Your job is very interesting. You ___ a lot of people.
I ___ plays. I often go to the theater.
Sam ___ a PC, but he doesn’t ___ a printer.
Do you ___ tea in the afternoon?
Sandy ___ hot dogs on Tuesdays.
Can I ___ my written test, please?

atoms.prompt: Write the sentence in the third person, as in the example.

items.stem: We live in a house. (she / flat)
They watch CNN. (Bob / MTV)
You study systems. (Erica / history)
I read love novels. (Henry / war novels)
We travel by United Airlines. (Mrs. Lane / British Airways)

accept: Mrs. Lane travels by British Airways.

atoms.prompt: Rewrite the sentence placing the frequency adverb in the right position.

items.stem: Mary drinks coffee in the morning. (always)

accept: Mary always drinks coffee in the morning.

items.stem: The teacher is in the office. (usually)

accept: The teacher is usually in the office.

items.stem: Bill and Bob study at night. (sometimes)

accept: Bill and Bob sometimes study at night.

items.stem: Sam reads French. (never)
They are in the laboratory. (seldom)

accept: They are seldom in the laboratory.

atoms.prompt: Put the sentences into the negative using the verb in brackets.

items.stem: A keyboard ___ in alphabetical order. (to be)
They ___ computers in their jobs. (to use)
The cursor ___ very slowly. (to move)
A mouse ___ ten keys. (to have)
The shop ___ free software. (to give)

atoms.prompt: Find the mistake and correct it.

items.stem: I leave always home at 7:00 a.m.

accept: I always leave home at 7:00 a.m.

items.stem: She never doesn’t go to UAI on Saturdays.

accept: She never goes to UAI on Saturdays.

items.stem: Do Bill sometimes study in the library?

accept: Does Bill sometimes study in the library?

items.stem: They don’t usually has a software class.

accept: They don't usually have a software class.
They usually have a software class.

source.note: La narrativa NO está en el material (link externo ausente). Escrita por nosotros con léxico de la U2, consistente con las respuestas oficiales: se levanta a las 7, mira TV a las 4, se va a dormir a las 9.

atoms.narrative: Hello. My name is Laura. I get up at seven o’clock every morning. I have breakfast and I go to work by bus. I work in an office and I usually have lunch at one. I leave work at three. In the afternoon, I watch TV at four. Then I make dinner and I read a book. I go to bed at nine because I am always tired.
atoms.speaker: narrator

questions.q: What time does she get up?
What does she do at nine o’clock?

source.section: Actividad de cierre — Composition My Life Chapter 2

atoms.prompt: COMPOSITION: MY LIFE: CHAPTER 2. Write about your daily routine: get up, have breakfast, go to work, have lunch, leave work, have dinner, go to bed.
atoms.modelAnswer: I get up at seven o’clock. I go to the bathroom and I have a shower. Then I have breakfast and I leave home. I take the bus to work and I get to work at nine. I usually have lunch at one. I leave work at five and I go home. I have dinner with my family and I sometimes watch TV. I go to bed at eleven.
modelVariants: I wake up early. I don’t have breakfast at home. I go to the university by train and I always arrive at eight. I have lunch in the canteen with my friends. In the afternoon I study and I often go running. At night I listen to music and I go to bed late.

rubric.text: Usa el presente simple en primera persona
Usa al menos un adverbio de frecuencia
Describe la rutina en orden (mañana → noche)

atoms.speaker: examiner

atoms.prompt: Rewrite the sentence replacing the phrase in italics with a frequency adverb.

items.stem: The teacher travels by bus most of the time.

accept: The teacher usually travels by bus.

items.stem: The French student almost never studies in the evening.

accept: The French student seldom studies in the evening.

items.stem: The Dean is in the university all the time.

accept: The Dean is always in the university.

items.stem: The Systems students don't attend classes on Saturdays.

accept: The Systems students never attend classes on Saturdays.

items.stem: The summer course students sit for their exams some of the time.

accept: The summer course students sometimes sit for their exams.

source.section: Ejercitación 7 (cuadro de la rutina de Peter)

atoms.prompt: Talk about Peter's weekly routine using the chart. Use frequency adverbs: always, never, sometimes, usually, once/twice/three times a week.
atoms.modelAnswer: Peter always leaves home at 7 a.m. He usually gets to school at 7:30. He sometimes has a morning meeting with the teachers. He usually has lunch in the canteen. He has an afternoon meeting twice a week. He always works in his office. He leaves work at 4 p.m. once a week. He never goes out with the students after work.
modelVariants: My mother always gets up at six. She usually goes to work by car. She sometimes has meetings in the morning. She has lunch at home twice a week. She never works on Sundays.

rubric.text: Usa expresiones de frecuencia (once/twice a week)
Describe la semana de Peter en orden

atoms.speaker: narrator
